entry.title: Wienerschnitzel Introduces New All-American Bigger, Better Burgers
entry.summary:
Wienerschnitzel upgrades its burger offerings for a limited time with the debut of new All-American Bigger, Better Burgers.
Featuring one or two grilled quarter-pound, beef smash burger patties and a toasted brioche bun, Wienerschnitzel's All-American Bigger, Better Burgers come in several varieties, including: - Cheeseburger - A grilled, beef smash burger patty, American cheese, mustard, ketchup, pickles, and grilled onions, served on a toasted brioche bun. - Chili Cheeseburger - A grilled, beef smash burger patty, American cheese, and Wienerschnitzel's signature Chili Sauce, served on a toasted brioche bun. - Jalapeno BBQ Bacon Cheeseburger - A grilled, beef smash burger patty, American cheese, bacon, jalapeno slices, jalapeno crisps, and BBQ sauce, served on a toasted brioche bun. - Deluxe Cheeseburger - A grilled, beef smash burger patty, American cheese, lettuce, tomato, diced onions, pickles, mayo, ketchup, and mustard, served on a toasted brioche bun. Prices vary, but the new burgers range from $5.49 (for a single Chili Cheeseburger) to $9.99 (for a Double Jalapeno BBQ Bacon Burger) here in Los Angeles. Photo via Wienerschnitzel. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Shake Shack Add Onion Rings to Permanent Menu
entry.summary:
Originally a limited-time menu item to accompany the French Onion Burger this fall-winter, Shake Shack has announced that it has added Onion Rings to its permanent menu.
Shake Shack's Onion Rings feature crispy-fried, beer-battered onion rings made from sweet Spanish onions served with your choice of sauce (ranch, honey mustard, BBQ, or Shack Sauce; cheese sauce is also available for an extra charge). Prices vary, but Onion Rings cost $5.49 at my local Shake Shack here in Los Angeles. Photo via Shake Shack. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Denny's Unveils New Sneakers Made with Syrup
entry.summary:
Denny's unveils new sneakers called "Sticky Kicks," which include a clear, sealed panel containing Denny's syrup. The diner chain will be selling a very limited number of the collectible shoes on National Maple Syrup Day, December 17, 2025, at 12PM EST exclusively on its Diner Drip online store.
The chain acknowledges that the new sneakers are "impractical, unnecessary, and completely over the top," but apparently that's the point. They were created in collaboration with custom sneaker artist and footwear designer Mache. In addition to the sealed syrup panel, the Sticky Kicks features syrup-colored patent leather, tumbled yellow accents, and the Denny's logo embossed on the heel. If you're hoping to score a pair, bear in mind that they will only be available in adult men's sizes 8 to 13 and cost $195 a pair. Additionally, Denny's Rewards members will get a special code to unlock the sale and place an order an hour earlier than anyone else. Photo via Denny's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Little Caesars Tests New Version of "More for $9.99" Deal
entry.summary:
Little Caesars is testing a new lineup of its "More for $9.99" promotion, potentially bringing it back in the future.
The new lineup is available for a limited time in participating locations in several markets, including Greenville-Spartanburg-Asheville, SC; Chattanooga, TN; and Harrisburg-York-Lancaster-Lebanon, PA. The "More for $9.99" test pairings include:
Large Classic Pizza (Cheese or Pepperoni) and Crazy Puffs (Pepperoni or 4 Cheese). Slices-N-Stix (Regular, Bacon, or Jalapeno) and Crazy Bread. Slices-N-Stix (Regular, Bacon, or Jalapeno) and a 2-liter soda bottle. Caesar Wings (Oven Roasted, BBQ, or Buffalo) and Crazy Bread. Pretzel Crust Pizza (Cheese or Pepperoni with Cheese Sauce) and Crazy Combo (Crazy Bread and Crazy Sauce).
Notable differences from the version offered during the summer include the lack of a two pizza option and the addition of Caesar Wings. Photo via Little Caesars. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Ben and Jerry's Reveals New Ice Cream Bars
entry.summary:
Ben & Jerry's reveals a new line of ice cream bars ahead of a January 2026 launch.
The new Ben & Jerry's Ice Cream Bars come in five flavors:
Caramel Blondie - Caramel ice cream with blondie pieces and salted caramel swirls, dipped in a white chocolaty coating with cookie pieces. Chocolate Fudge Brownie - Chocolate ice cream with fudge brownie pieces, dipped in a chocolaty coating with chocolate cookie pieces. Cookie Dough - Vanilla ice cream with chocolate chip cookie dough chunks, dipped in a chocolaty coating with cookie pieces. PB Pretzel - Peanut butter ice cream with peanut butter swirls, dipped in a chocolaty coating with pretzel pieces. Strawberry Cheesecake - Cheesecake ice cream with strawberry swirls, dipped in a white chocolaty coating with graham cracker pieces.
The new bars will be available in a box containing four 2.5-oz bars with a suggested price range of $5.99 to $7.49. Individual Cookie Dough Ben & Jerry's Ice Cream Bars will also be rolled out to convenience stores next spring for $3.99 each. As a promotion for the new product launch, Ben and Jerry's will be giving out the new ice cream bars in Venice Beach, CA, on Saturday, December 13, 2025, from 1PM to 5PM. Additionally, 150 participating Ben & Jerry's Scoop Shops nationwide will be giving out ice cream bars, while supplies last, on Wednesday, December 17, 2025. This is actually the third time Ben & Jerry's has put out a line of ice cream bars. They previously introduced Pint Slices in 2017 and offered various bars throughout the 1980s and '90s. They were all eventually discontinued. Photo via Ben & Jerry's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Chick-fil-A Tests New Chicken & Waffles Sandwiches
entry.summary:
Chick-fil-A is testing new Chicken & Waffles Sandwiches for a limited time through January 24, 2026, while supplies last.
There are two varieties being tested: The Chicken & Waffles Breakfast Sandwich and the Chicken & Waffles Sandwich. The former is being tested at participating locations in Baltimore, MD and San Antonio, TX, while the latter is only available in Baltimore. The Chicken & Waffles Breakfast Sandwich is only available during breakfast hours and features a breakfast portion of a chicken filet (Original, spicy, or grilled) served with applewood-smoked bacon and honey butter spread on maple-flavored waffles. A side of syrup is included. The Chicken & Waffles Sandwich is only available outside of breakfast hours (from 10:30 AM to close) is the same as the breakfast sandwich only with a regular-size chicken filet (as with the breakfast version, you can get it with an Original, spicy, or a grilled filet) rather than a breakfast portion. Prices vary, but a location in Baltimore charges $6.05 to $6.29 for the Chicken & Waffles Breakfast Sandwich and $9.29 to $10.09 for the Chicken & Waffles Sandwich. You can also purchase just the waffle for $1.89. Photo via Chick-fil-A. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Mars' 2025 Holiday Lineup Includes New M&M's Winter Blend
entry.summary:
Mars Wrigley introduces new M&M's Winter Blend and more for its 2025 holiday lineup, which is available now for a limited time in store nationwide.
New M&M'S Winter Blend are classic Milk Chocolate M&M's, but with frosty white and blizzard blue candy shells. Also new for the season are: - Twix Snowmen - The signature combination of crunchy cookie, caramel and milk chocolate in the shape of a snowman for the holidays. - Life Savers Gummies Holiday Shapes - Soft and fruity Life Savers Gummies also get some seasonal shapes this year, including stockings, snowmen, and Santa hats. In addition to the new festive products, Mars Wrigley also brings back several candies for the holiday season, including: - M&M'S Holiday Blend - A festive mix of red and green chocolate candies available in Peanut, Peanut Butter, and Milk Chocolate varieties. - Snickers Trees - The classic combination of caramel, nougat, roasted peanuts and milk chocolate, but in the shape of a tree. - M&M'S Toasty Holiday Peanut - Candy coasted, milk chocolate covered roasted peanuts infused with cinnamon, vanilla, spiced sugar, and caramel flavors. - Skittles Original Candy Holiday Cane - The plastic cane comes filled with classic strawberry, orange, grape, lemon and green apple flavors. Photo via Mars Wrigley. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Domino's Offers Free Chocolate Lava Crunch Cakes with $10+ Orders Through December 22, 2025
entry.summary:
Domino's celebrates its 65th birthday by offering a limited-time deal where you can get a free order of Chocolate Lava Crunch Cakes with any online order of $10 or more through December 22, 2025.
To take advantage of the birthday special, you'll need to use the code "HAPPYBDAY" when ordering through the Domino's mobile app or website. Each order of Chocolate Lava Crunch Cakes includes three chocolate cakes with molten chocolate fudge on the inside, dusted on top with powdered sugar. Prices vary, but an order normally costs $9.99 at my local Domino's here in Los Angeles (you can check out my review of them from when an order of two cost $3.99). Photo via Domino's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Sonic Tests New Cold Coffee Lineup
entry.summary:
Sonic looks to reinvigorate its coffee offerings with a limited-time test of its new cold coffee lineup at participating locations in Denver, CO; Grand Junction, CO; Joplin, MO; Las Vegas, NV; New York, NY; and Monroe, LA.
The new lineup includes: - Iced Coffee - "Smooth, sweetened coffee poured over Sonic ice." - Cold Foam Iced Coffee - Iced coffee topped with cold foam. - Iced Coffee Latte - Iced coffee mixed with creamer and sugar. - Coffee Chiller - Vanilla soft serve blended with coffee, topped with whipped topping. The new coffee beverages are available in 16-oz and 20-oz sizes. Prices start at $2.49. Each drink is available in four varieties: Original, Caramel, Vanilla, and Oreo. The Caramel and Vanilla varieties of each drink add caramel syrup and vanilla syrup, respectively, but the Oreo version of each drinks varies a little: the Oreo Iced Coffee adds Oreo syrup; the Oreo Iced Coffee with Cold Foam adds Oreo syrup and Oreo cookie crumbles on top; the Oreo Iced Coffee Latte adds Oreo syrup and comes crowned with whipped topping and Oreo crumbles; and the Oreo Coffee Chiller blends in Oreo syrup and Oreo crumbles and tops it all with whipped topping and Oreo crumbles. You can check the Sonic app or website to see if the test beverages are available near you. Photo via Sonic. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: White Castle Tests New Cheddar Smoked Sausage Slider and Pretzel Nibblers
entry.summary:
White Castle is testing new Cheddar Smoked Sausage Sliders and Pretzel Nibblers for a limited time in Cincinnati, Louisville, and New Jersey, to see if they merit a turn on the chain's national menu.
The Cheddar Smoked Sausage Slider features a split smoked sausage link infused with cheddar cheese, served with caramelized onions on a slider bun. New Pretzel Nibblers are fried, soft pretzel nuggets tossed in kosher salt and served with a warm and creamy cheese sauce. Prices vary, but a Cheddar Smoked Sausage Slider costs $1.29 at a White Castle restaurant in Louisville, while a 6-piece order of Pretzel Nibblers are $2.99. Photo via White Castle. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Jersey Mike's Tests New Hot Italian Sub
entry.summary:
Jersey Mike's is testing its new Mike's Hot Italian sub sandwich for a limited time at select locations in four markets.
The new sub features ham, salami & pepperoni lightly grilled with melted provolone cheese, served with grilled onions, lettuce, tomato, and Hot Chopped Pepper Relish on your choice of bread. Mike's Hot Italian is a hot and spicy twist on the chain's Original Italian sandwich. It is being tested in the areas around Minneapolis, Salt Lake City, San Diego, and Chicago A regular Mike's Hot Italian sandwich costs $11.95 in San Diego. Photo via Jersey Mike's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Crumbl Debuts New Cookies & Cream Cocoa Mousse Cup
entry.summary:
Crumbl welcomes the new Cookies & Cream Cocoa Mousse Cup as part of this week's limited-time menu available from December 8 through December 13, 2025.
Here's what this week's national lineup at Crumbl looks like: - New Cookies & Cream Cocoa Mousse Cup - Layers of cocoa mousse, cookies & cream streusel, and dark chocolate cake, crowned with marshmallow mousse, toasted marshmallows, a chocolate drizzle, and cookies & cream pieces around the rim. - New Holiday M&M's Cookie - A warm cookie packed with a holiday blend of M&M's Milk Chocolate Candies. - Turtle Cheesecake - A cheesecake on a cookies & cream crust, topped with ganache, caramel, and crunchy pecan pieces. - Holiday Birthday Cake Cookie - A warm cake batter cookie topped with cake batter cream cheese frosting and festive sprinkles. - S'mores Brownie Cookie - A warm cookie mix of brownie, graham cracker, and milk chocolate chips, topped with and melted marshmallows. - Molten Lava Cookie - A warm, dark chocolate cookie with a center of hot fudge, dusted with powdered sugar. - Semi-sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A warm cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and flaky sea salt. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Review: Shake Shack - Big Shack
entry.summary:
Shake Shack's Big Shack is a limited-time, Big Mac-like burger that features two quarter-pound Angus beef patties, secret sauce, green leaf lettuce, two slices of American cheese, Roma tomatoes, thick-cut pickles, and onions, on three buttered and toasted potato buns.
The Big Shack normally costs $9.99, but I used the chain's current deal, which lets you get one for free when you spend $10 or more. I bought a Coffee Shake and onion rings for a little over $10.
My particular Big Shack came with an almost comically small top bun and threatened to topple over due to its height and the very thick, lightly-pickled cucumbers and other produce.
It looked pretty good. I had a bit of a debate with myself over whether to try to cut it in half for a cross-section shot. I managed, though:
The "secret sauce" was just Shack Sauce. Compared to Mac Sauce, it's more subtle (less tangy, less sweet) and savory. The middle bun was pointless. It might have been useful if it had been between the pickles and tomatoes to help hold them in place. The melted cheese would have been plenty enough to hold the patties. The lone leaf of lettuce didn't do much for me. The beef patties were cooked really well. They had crispy edges, a nice sear on both sides, and a flavorful, juicy interior.
The onion rings I got on the side were excellent. They had a nice, crispy beer-battered shell and were cooked through for an easy, clean bite.
At the end of the day, I really enjoyed the Big Shack. It's basically a Double ShackBurger with a middle bun, onions, and pickles at a slight discount (much more so with the deal). While it's somewhat unwieldy to eat due to its height and construction, the tastes and textures were great, and the onion and pickles added a nice crunch, accompanied by light flavors. Read more at Brand Eating!

author.name: Brand Eating. Your Daily Fast Food Reading.
